message:
[LOW] fcp-mesh: close verification gap from 59f21047 / 739add27

Cross-review of the Apr 20 fcp-mesh hardening set (59f21047 lease renew,
739add27 transcript binding, e269d9e1 audit summary) verified the three
claimed commits compile and pass tests. 59f21047 explicitly flagged a
"Verification gap: cargo check/test could not complete in this session"
— that gap masked five concrete regressions now fixed here:

1. node.rs (3 sites) — tests constructed fcp_core::NodeSignature::new(peer, ...)
   where peer is fcp_tailscale::NodeId. Post the pizld NodeId reconciliation,
   NodeSignature::new takes fcp_core::NodeId, so the three gossip-summary
   and revocation-push signing tests failed to compile. Wrapped each call
   in fcp_core::NodeId::new(peer.as_str()) to match the signing path used
   elsewhere in the suite (mesh_integration.rs:5536).

2. planner.rs — placement_evidence_from_plan_with_selected asserted
   evidence.connector_id.as_str() == "fcp.test", but ConnectorId::new
   renders "fcp:test:1.0.0". Assertion flipped to the actual canonical
   form; evidence_from_plan just clones connector_id through, so this is
   purely a stale test expectation.

3. mesh_integration.rs — decode_complete_stops_transfer_via_meshnode
   built a DecodeStatus with recipient_node_id="node-repair" against a
   MeshNode constructed as "node-decode". 739add27's new recipient-binding
   check (RecipientMismatch) correctly rejects that and makes the test
   fail as written. Pointed the recipient at "node-decode" so the test
   actually exercises the decode-complete stop path, which is its stated
   purpose.

4. mesh_integration.rs — test_zone_boundary_enforcement created a node
   with zones=vec![] and a PlannerContext with a target_zone, then
   asserted candidates was non-empty. ExecutionPlanner::check_target_zone
   now explicitly drops nodes with unknown zone membership (ZoneRestriction
   penalty + mark_ineligible). Since the test name is literally
   "zone_boundary_enforcement", flipped the assertion to require an empty
   candidate set — the original assertion was asserting the opposite of
   enforcement.

5. multi_node_harness.rs (2 sites) — same NodeSignature::new type fix
   as (1) for send_revocation_push and the in-task summary signing path.

Residual MEDIUM finding (documented, not patched):

  qvzvp — 739add27 added recipient_node_id + request_nonce to the
  SymbolAck / DecodeStatus signed transcripts (V1→V2) and wired
  recipient_node_id binding into handle_* on the receive side. The
  request_nonce field is present in the signed bytes but no handler
  (process_symbol_ack, process_decode_status) verifies it against an
  issued-nonce set, and SymbolRequest itself carries no request_nonce
  for the responder to echo. That leaves within-recipient same-object
  cross-transfer replay open: an old signed ack for completed transfer
  T1 can cancel a later transfer T2 of the same object, because
  process_symbol_ack keys purely on object_id and active_transfers[X]
  is re-populated by track_transfer() at T2 start. Proper fix needs a
  SymbolRequest.request_nonce field + TransferState tracking + echo
  check — architectural, outside a narrow patch pass, and qvzvp already
  tracks it.

Residual LOW findings (unchanged from e269d9e1):

  - MeshGossip::handle_summary is pub and re-exported; doc comment tells
    callers to verify signature first but there's no type-level
    enforcement. No external callers in-tree; flag if/when an out-of-tree
    crate reaches it.
  - MeshNode::register_peer_signing_key silently overwrites on repeat
    calls. Trust-anchor rotation policy should be explicit (distinct
    register vs. replace_key APIs, or at least a warn-log on overwrite).

Also observed, not chased further at session close:

  - multi_node_harness_enforces_summary_signature_boundary asserts
    signed_snapshot.peer_count == 1 but the harness surfaces 2. Does not
    repro in the signature path I changed (NodeSignature node_id field
    is string-compared so the fcp_tailscale → fcp_core wrap is inert).
    Pre-existing harness bookkeeping issue; not touched.

Test status after this commit on fcp-mesh:
  --lib:   1174 pass / 0 fail
  --tests: remaining failure in multi_node_harness as noted above;
           mesh_integration fully green; no_mock_integration fully green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
